## Harmonising and comparing PHQ-9 vs Beck Anxiety Inventory


<!-- BEGIN BOILERPLATE -->

Harmony is an AI tool which uses Large Language Models to automatically match items from different questionnaires, even across different languages. You can try Harmony here: https://harmonydata.ac.uk/app. Harmony is also available as a [Python](https://pypi.org/project/harmonydata/) and [R package](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/harmonydata/index.html).

You may also be interested in [Harmony Meta](https://harmonydata.ac.uk/search/), our new discovery engine that helps you search through vast catalogues of study metadata to identify the variables you need for your research.

<!-- END BOILERPLATE -->

## Compare the items in PHQ-9 and Beck Anxiety Inventory

* <a href="https://harmonydata.ac.uk/app/#" target="harmonyapp">Import PHQ-9 and Beck Anxiety Inventory into Harmony and harmonise them</a> - find similarities between the two instruments using our AI platform
* <a href="https://harmonydata.ac.uk/app/#" target="harmonyapp">Import just PHQ-9 into Harmony</a> - compare PHQ-9 with other instruments
* <a href="https://harmonydata.ac.uk/app/#" target="harmonyapp">Import just Beck Anxiety Inventory into Harmony</a> - compare Beck Anxiety Inventory with other instruments


## Other instruments which you can compare and harmonise with Harmony

* [Other psychological instruments in areas such as depression and anxiety](/compare-harmonise-instruments)
* [Download Beck Anxiety Inventory in PDF form](https://res.cloudinary.com/dpmykpsih/image/upload/great-plains-health-site-358/media/1087/anxiety.pdf)

## References

* Beck, A. T., Epstein, N., Brown, G., & Steer, R. (1988). Beck Anxiety Inventory [Database record]. APA PsycTests. https://doi.org/10.1037/t02025-000
